_meta.description: Page manifest for the focal-4 HI viewer. Each entry corresponds to one HI page-side (left or right half of a 2-page PDF spread). The 'sequence' field is the navigation order in the viewer (left-to-right within a domain, then next domain). The 'book_page' is the printed page number visible at the bottom margin of the page.

pages.page_header_kanji: 藩制一覧表第二 (鹿兒島)
pages.page_summary_en: The Satsuma (Kagoshima) entry's Table 2 page shows the kokudaka aggregates: sōdaka of 869,594 koku (with a 琉球國高引除 annotation indicating Ryūkyū tribute is excluded), the breakdown into 損高 (loss-high) and the remaining 高, plus current rice yield (現石) and zatsuzei (miscellaneous tax) figures including the Ryūkyū sugar monopoly revenue. The kokudaka figure cross-validated against the 2026-05-27 crop verification.

pages.page_header_kanji: 藩制一覧表第八 (鹿児島)
pages.page_topic: officers + military, with explicit non-disclosure annotation
pages.page_summary_en: Satsuma's HI Table 8 entry is essentially a non-disclosure: just the ○鹿兒島藩 marker followed by 書ナラス ('not specified / not written'). The numeric values currently showing in the data table (兵卒人員 1,568; 士等役員 1,106; 隊士 464; officer roster 47) sit to the RIGHT of the 鹿兒島藩 marker on this page — which in R→L Japanese reading order means they're read BEFORE Satsuma's marker, and therefore belong to the PRECEDING domain whose entry continues onto this page from vol 2 PDF p162 (outside the focal-4 sample). MASTER currently mis-attributes those values to Satsuma; this is flagged as a data-integrity issue for separate correction. Satsuma's true disclosure on this page is the 書ナラス note — a near-total refusal that, combined with their actual ~16,000-Spencer-rifle-armed force, made them the most opaque military presence in the 1870 survey.

The Chōshū demographics page uses the 内 X 人口 sub-row pattern (内 士族 人口 = 15,849; 内 卒族 人口 = 10,362) introducing samurai-class population as sub-counts of a total. This labeling pattern was the gap that caused Chōshū's HI shizoku/sotsu data to be missing from the analytic dataset until the 2026-05-27 build-script fix. The page also shows the total 戸數 (17,714) and the city-subset 士族戸數 (narrow / city subset, 58 households).

The Chōshū kokudaka page documents 草高 = 369,411 + 外高 (新田打出) = 628,593 + 合高 = 988,004. Internal arithmetic note: 369,411 + 628,593 = 998,004, not 988,004 — the HI source itself has a 10,000-koku arithmetic inconsistency on this page (likely a printing error in the 1870 edition). MASTER captures the source faithfully (verbatim values, including the inconsistency). The kokudaka_iv uses the 988,004 combined figure as printed by HI.

pages.page_topic: kokudaka + 5-year average + 戸數 total + product list
pages.page_summary_en: Tosa's Table 2 page shows the 5-year-average kokudaka (草高 495,486; current rice 現米 189,431; tax sub-categories including 茅蠟 wax and 海藻 seaweed products); 戸數 (93,736 total). The 5-year averaging annotation 甲子ヨリ戊辰 (from Kashi to Boshin = 1864–1868) covers the run-up to and through the Boshin War.

pages.page_topic: shizoku and sotsu persons + households + eta/hinin sub-counts
pages.page_summary_en: Tosa's demographic detail page: 士族 35,068 persons (with M+F sub-totals 15,368 + 19,700 = 35,068 internally consistent ✓), 卒族 17,681 persons, 士族戸數 7,269, 卒族戸數 3,713, plus 穢多戸數 3,396 and other status-class sub-counts. The shizoku ratio (53 persons per 100 households) is unusually high, reflecting Tosa's large gōshi (郷士) samurai class.

pages.page_topic: kokudaka via 'two-mouth combined / 5-year average' + main tax rice
pages.page_summary_en: Saga's kokudaka entry uses an unusual phrasing: 貮口合 平均 ('two-mouth combined, average') = 724,739.3152 koku, which is the 5-year average of (高 211,519 + 新田高 513,580). This is Saga's equivalent of 合高. The 正租 米 = 213,478 koku and 取納現米 = 217,789 koku also appear here. Saga is the only focal-4 domain entered ONLY in vol2 (no vol1 entry); the 'gap' is documented in memory reference_saga_HI_gap.

pages.page_header_kanji: 藩制一覧表第六 (佐嘉) / 藩制一覧表第六 (佐倉)
pages.page_header_en: Domain Survey Table 6 — STACKED: Saga top half / Sakura-han bottom half
pages.page_summary_en: This page is STACKED — the top half completes Saga's entry (士族人員 23,823 + 卒人員 43,488 + 卒戸數 9,369 + 僧人員 4,412), and the bottom half begins ○佐倉藩 (Sakura-han, a Sakai domain in Shimousa) as a NEW DOMAIN. The ○ marker at mid-page is the source's domain-boundary signal. Annotations distinguish the two domains' data.
